texto: For me, and this may not be everybody, but because I do love country music so much, there's such a feeling of home in Nashville, especially because it's such a small town. You bring up one song, everybody knows who wrote it, everybody knows their mother and what their cell number is, and all of the stories.
explicacao: Citação de Garrett Hedlund sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “For me, and this may not be everybody, but because I do love country music so much, there's such …” articula familia, love, music em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como music e mother.

You bring up one song, everybody knows who wrote it, everybody knows their mother and what their cell number is, and all of the stories.

texto: I was a 'Duck Hunt' and 'Mario' guy, and stuff like that. I was never technologically driven. I never had all the cool, new toys. I was the youngest child, I wasn't the only child, so I wasn't spoiled as a kid. And, we were on the farm, so we didn't have a lot. Also, with computers, I'm not very good with them. I just check my email.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Garrett Hedlund: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I was a 'Duck Hunt' and 'Mario' guy, and stuff like that. I was never technologically driven. I n…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, good, cool pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de cool e child.

texto: I remember telling my creative writing teacher that you never want to have a journal, because if you lose it, then someone's going to know all your secrets. And then she stopped using a journal, but I always write everything down... Anytime I travel, I try and fill up notepads.
explicacao: Citação de Garrett Hedlund sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I remember telling my creative writing teacher that you never want to have a journal, because if …” articula educacao, travel, writing em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como viagem e writing.

texto: See, the 'On the Road' that came out in 1957 was censored. A lot of the honesty of it, the bitter honesty, is in the original scroll version that came out in 2007 on the 50-year anniversary. Back then, there was so much post-Second World War fear that was imposed on everybody - 'You must live life this way' - and these guys were bored.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Garrett Hedlund sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. A afirmação “See, the 'On the Road' that came out in 1957 was censored. A lot of the honesty of it, the bitter…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, anniversary, fear, útil em momentos de viagem e anniversary.

texto: You might think the thinner version of yourself is going to be the most positive or confident, but that's not how it is for me. When I'm over 200 pounds, that's when I'm the most confident version of myself.
